title: An Enterotoxin-Bearing Pathogenicity Island in Staphylococcus epidermidis
summary: The paper describes the novel bacteriophage φ909 from Staphylococcus epidermidis FRI909, characterized by transmission electron microscopy as having icosahedral heads (~60 nm diameter) and flexible banded tails with end plates. It also mentions several other staphylococcal phages (φSPβ, φ11, φ13, 80α, φPVL108, ϕSLT, ϕN315, ϕ12, 187, 52A) in comparative genomic contexts, but provides no morphological details for them.

φ909.morphology: symmetrical icosahedral heads, flexible banded tails, and end plates
φ909.structural_proteins: portal protein (GSEF_1844), major capsid protein (GSEF_1843), tail tape length measure protein (GSEF_1835), minor capsid protein (GSEF_1830), major tail protein (GSEF_1838), minor structural protein (GSEF_1833), acylhydrolase putative structural component (GSEF_1831), phage infection protein (GSEF_1825)

extra.figure_caption: Fig. 4. Sequence and ORF analysis of the temperate bacteriophage φ909 in S. epidermidis FRI909. (A) The φ909 bacteriophage insertion is found immediately downstream of the FemC/glutamine synthetase gene (SERP0876 in RP62a; GSEF_0257 in FRI909), a site distinct from the composite genomic island containing SePI-1. The genome shows conserved regions corresponding to those seen in S. aureus phages. (B) Ultrastructure of the bacteriophage showing intact phage particles. Transmission electron microscopy reveals isometric heads (diameter, 60 nm) with long, banded tails and end plates. Arrows mark end plates on tails. (C) PCR amplification of toxin genes from phage DNA and bacterial genomic DNA (gDNA). Lanes 1 and 4, gyrA; lanes 2 and 5, the SEC3 gene; lanes 3 and 6, porF; lane M, marker.
extra.tail_description: long, banded tails with end plates
